text:
Market today: Tech carried the first half and the mood is still constructive - AI spending headlines (UAE $49B fund, Anthropic export restrictions lifted) are keeping chip and software names bid. That's your world, so the wind is at your back today.

Your holdings: Everything is green and above stops, so no fires to put out. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged ETF - it moves violently and decays over time, so your $200 stop is wide on a leveraged name; worth tightening if you're on margin. SPAL is brand-new and very volatile - tiny position sizing matters because margin amplifies any gap-down hard.

Watch: Whether NVDA can hold the $198 area - it's the bellwether for everything you own. Also watch if the market closes early Friday (July 4th holiday); thin volume can whipsaw leveraged names.

Bottom line: You're in good shape but leveraged across the board - protect gains by keeping stops honest, not by adding size.
plays: SOXL: With a 3x leveraged ETF up 4.8% on margin, consider tightening your stop above $200 toward the $220-230 area to lock in more of the move.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, very volatile - keep position small and respect the $21.38 stop hard; margin makes a gap-down extremely painful.
No new buys today - NVDA scanner setup (entry $198.39-198.62, stop $197.35) is only grade C with 4H pointing down; not compelling enough to add tech exposure when you're already heavily leveraged.
